13:
To fight in Reunion is forbidden, and to break the peace is to be banished forever. This he says without words, for words are lies to the Qestir.
They do not speak.

To fight in Reunion is forbidden, and to break the peace is to be banished forever. This he says without words, for words are lies to the Qestir.
They do not speak.

23:
Not all seek the Dawn Throne. Some are satisfied with their lot.
Others, like the Qestir, have reason to remain neutral.

Not all seek the Dawn Throne. Some are satisfied with their lot.
Others, like the Qestir, have reason to remain neutral.

30:
The time is ripe, my lord.
Return with us, blade in hand, and lead Doma to freedom.

The time is ripe, my lord.
Return with us, blade in hand, and lead Doma to freedom.

38:
There is no doubting your strength, nor your character.
It would be my honor to fight by your side.

There is no doubting your strength, nor your character.
It would be my honor to fight by your side.

45:
'Twould be folly to challenge them without first supplementing our forces.
So yes, I intend to win the Naadam and enlist the aid of every able-bodied
Xaela I can!

'Twould be folly to challenge them without first supplementing our forces.
So yes, I intend to win the Naadam and enlist the aid of every able-bodied
Xaela I can!

61:
To that end, I wish to fight in the Naadam─as a warrior of the Mol.
Will you grant me this honor?

To that end, I wish to fight in the Naadam─as a warrior of the Mol.
Will you grant me this honor?

67:
Hear me, then.
As khatun of the Mol, I invite you all to join with us in the Naadam.

Hear me, then.
As khatun of the Mol, I invite you all to join with us in the Naadam.

71:
A singular radiance. Shimmering.
Like a jewel of the Dusk Mother, blinding in its brilliance.

A singular radiance. Shimmering.
Like a jewel of the Dusk Mother, blinding in its brilliance.

74:
Cherish the stars and the light they bring you in the dark.
For you are a traveler, are you not?

Cherish the stars and the light they bring you in the dark.
For you are a traveler, are you not?

85:
You are none the worse for your time in the wilderness. On the contrary,
I would say you have improved.

You are none the worse for your time in the wilderness. On the contrary,
I would say you have improved.

89:
Instead they have called for the sword, and so that is what I must be.
Until this blade is broken, I can but carve a path forward.

Instead they have called for the sword, and so that is what I must be.
Until this blade is broken, I can but carve a path forward.

90:
...Were you truly so concerned for my well-being?
You never showed it when we used to spar.

...Were you truly so concerned for my well-being?
You never showed it when we used to spar.

93:
A man should not be addressed by his childhood name.
I've told you about that before!

A man should not be addressed by his childhood name.
I've told you about that before!

115:
Whatever happens, we shouldn't keep Cirina and the others waiting.
Let's be ready to make a swift exit, all right?

Whatever happens, we shouldn't keep Cirina and the others waiting.
Let's be ready to make a swift exit, all right?

122:
When I learned of trespassers, I bade my warriors take their measure.
To flay them if they failed.

When I learned of trespassers, I bade my warriors take their measure.
To flay them if they failed.

135:
How he died matters not.
Geser was a great warrior. He will return to us in time.

How he died matters not.
Geser was a great warrior. He will return to us in time.

148:
But the soul lives on, they say...these bodies no more than empty husks returning to the earth. Seen through those eyes, I suppose the sight
is almost comforting.

But the soul lives on, they say...these bodies no more than empty husks returning to the earth. Seen through those eyes, I suppose the sight
is almost comforting.

151:
A samurai will die for his lord without hesitation or regret.
But this is not because he delights in such sacrifice.

A samurai will die for his lord without hesitation or regret.
But this is not because he delights in such sacrifice.

157:
'Tis little wonder the Oronir won the last Naadam.
These people leave naught to chance.

'Tis little wonder the Oronir won the last Naadam.
These people leave naught to chance.

170:
But my father did, as did many of my fallen comrades.
As do many of my people now.

But my father did, as did many of my fallen comrades.
As do many of my people now.

183:
Thank you, my friends.
Upon the soil of one and all shall our deeds be inscribed...

Thank you, my friends.
Upon the soil of one and all shall our deeds be inscribed...

190:
We did it... And you, my friend...you...you were magnificent.
Magnificent...and, uh...not a little terrifying.

We did it... And you, my friend...you...you were magnificent.
Magnificent...and, uh...not a little terrifying.

198:
You forget to whom you speak, Doman. We are the undying ones.
We are Dotharl.

You forget to whom you speak, Doman. We are the undying ones.
We are Dotharl.

206:
As if we'd lose to the likes of him! And we're only gonna get stronger. Stronger and stronger until we're strong enough to take back everything
they stole!

As if we'd lose to the likes of him! And we're only gonna get stronger. Stronger and stronger until we're strong enough to take back everything
they stole!

213:
Very well. The battlefield matters not, nor else the enemy.
In battle do our souls burn bright.

Very well. The battlefield matters not, nor else the enemy.
In battle do our souls burn bright.

215:
The Sun shall answer your call, Doman.
Wild as the wind the udgan and her brood may be, but they will deliver you naught. We will deliver you your kingdom.

The Sun shall answer your call, Doman.
Wild as the wind the udgan and her brood may be, but they will deliver you naught. We will deliver you your kingdom.

224:
Alphinaud and Alisaie Leveilleur, I presume? I am told we owe you much.
It is a pleasure to meet you both.

Alphinaud and Alisaie Leveilleur, I presume? I am told we owe you much.
It is a pleasure to meet you both.
